titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 618

p.seg: Alexander by the grace of God, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justiciars, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all other worthy men of the whole of his land sends greeting.

Let present and future know us, moved by God's love, to have given, granted, and by this our charter to have made firm to God and Blessed Mary and Saint Cuthbert and Thomas the prior of Coldingham, and to the monks in that place serving God now and for ever in the future, the twenty annual merks which we and our ancestors were accustomed to receive from Coldinghamshire as waiting. For the same monks and their successors for ever to have, receive, and hold, in free, pure and perpetual alms, as freely, quitely, fully, and honourably as the same monks freely, quitely, fully, and honourably hold and possess any alms.

As witnesses, Adam, abbot of Melrose, William of Bondington, chancellor, Walter son of Alan, steward, justiciar of Scotia, Patrick son of Earl Patrick, John of Maxwell, chamberlain, Roger Avenel, David Marshall, Eymer of Maxwell, John of Stirling,
at Berwick on the sixteenth day of May, in the eighteenth year of the lord king's reign [1232].
